Title: Address Popup Opens From Cart Shipping Details — Happy Path
Test_Data: User logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}} with at least 1 saved address; cart has at least 1 item
Steps: url={{FRONT_URL}}, user={{USER_EMAIL}}, password={{USER_PASSWORD}}
Assertions:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] cart page loaded with at least 1 item
[ACT] locate 'Shipping Details' section
[ACT] click 'Please select a shipping address' link or pencil icon next to an assigned address
[WAIT] address selection popup opens
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[DOM] address selection popup/modal is visible
[DOM] popup contains keyword search input (data-test-id="search-keyword-input")
[DOM] 3 facet dropdowns visible: Country (data-test-id="filter-country"), State (data-test-id="filter-region"), City (data-test-id="filter-city")
[DOM] address list renders at least one address row
[DOM] popup has 4 columns: Address / Description / Country / Active
Failure_Signals:
[API] currentCustomerAddresses query called; response errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no JS errors on popup open
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on /graphql
Cleanup: popup does not open; popup opens blank; spinner persists >5s; JS TypeError in console
References: Close popup without selecting address (press Esc or click X)

Title: Address Popup — Keyword Search Returns Filtered Results
Preconditions: User logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}} with 3+ saved addresses; address popup open
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] cart page loaded
[ACT] open address selection popup (click 'Please select a shipping address' or pencil icon)
[WAIT] address list renders
[ACT] click search input (data-test-id="search-keyword-input")
[ACT] type partial city or street value matching exactly 1 of the saved addresses
[WAIT] list refreshes with filtered results
[ACT] click magnifying-glass submit button
[WAIT] results updated
Assertions:
[DOM] address list shows only records matching the keyword
[COUNT] result count reduced compared to full list
[DOM] matching address row is visible
[DOM] non-matching addresses are absent
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] currentCustomerAddresses query called with keyword arg; errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during search
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on /graphql search request
Failure_Signals: list unchanged after typing keyword; spinner visible >5s; all addresses disappear (over-filter); console TypeError

Title: Address Popup — Keyword Search Clear Restores Full List
Preconditions: User logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}} with 3+ saved addresses; address popup open with active keyword filter
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] cart page loaded
[ACT] open address selection popup
[WAIT] address list renders
[ACT] type keyword in search input and submit
[WAIT] filtered results appear
[ACT] clear the search input (delete all text)
[WAIT] list refreshes
Assertions:
[DOM] full unfiltered address list restored
[COUNT] address count returns to the original count before filtering
[DOM] search input is empty
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] currentCustomerAddresses called with empty/absent keyword; errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no JS errors on clear
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: list stays filtered after clearing input; full list not restored; extra network call fails; empty state shown despite addresses existing

Title: Address Popup — Country Facet Filters Address List

Title: Address Popup — City Facet Shows City Name NOT Postal Code (Defect #2 Verification)
Preconditions: SEED REQUIRED (OQ-5) + Defect #2 must be fixed: City facet options must show city names. test org has addresses with known cities. OQ-2: confirm defect #2 fix is in scope before running
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/cart logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}
[WAIT] cart loaded
[ACT] open address selection popup
[WAIT] address list renders
[ACT] click 'City' facet dropdown (data-test-id="filter-city")
[WAIT] city options appear
Assertions:
[DOM] city facet option labels show city names (e.g. 'New York') — NOT postal codes (e.g. '10001')
[DOM] each city option shows count badge with positive integer
[COUNT] city option count matches number of addresses with that city
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] currentCustomerAddresses called; term_facets[].terms[].label contains city name string; errors[] is empty
[API] term_facets[].terms[].term contains the city identifier (separate from label)
[CONSOLE] no JS errors
Failure_Signals: city facet shows numeric postal codes instead of city names (Defect #2 regression); facet empty; spinner >5s

Title: Address Popup — Combined Facets (Country + State + City) Narrow Results
Preconditions: SEED REQUIRED (OQ-5): addresses in multiple countries/states/cities; address popup open
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/cart logged in as {{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}
[WAIT] cart loaded
[ACT] open address selection popup
[WAIT] address list renders
[ACT] click 'Country' facet and select 'United States'
[WAIT] list filters
[ACT] click 'State' facet and select a specific state
[WAIT] list filters further
[ACT] click 'City' facet and select a specific city
[WAIT] list filters further
Assertions:
[DOM] each successive facet selection further reduces the address list
[COUNT] final result count matches addresses in US + selected state + selected city
[DOM] all 3 facets show their selected values
[DOM] results contain only addresses matching all 3 criteria
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] currentCustomerAddresses called with all 3 args (countryCodes, regionIds, cities); errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no JS errors on combined filter
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: applying second facet clears first; combined filter returns zero results despite matches; API called without all 3 args

Title: Address Popup — Column Sort NOT Wired (Defect #1 Bug Verification)
Preconditions: Build deployed to QA before Defect #1 fix. Verify column header is NOT interactive (cursor: auto). After fix is deployed this test becomes a PASS verification for sort functionality.
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] cart loaded
[ACT] open address selection popup
[WAIT] address list renders
[ACT] attempt to click column header 'Address'
[WAIT] observe any sort behavior
Assertions:
[DOM] column header has aria-sort="none" attribute
[DOM] column header has cursor: auto style (NOT cursor: pointer) — sort is NOT wired
[STATE] address list order does NOT change after clicking column header
[DOM] no sort indicator arrows appear
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no click handler fires on column header click; no JS error from unhandled sort
[NETWORK] no additional /graphql request on column header click
Failure_Signals: column sort fires and reorders list (would mean Defect #1 is fixed — update test); TypeError on column click

Title: Address Popup — Search Case-Insensitive Matching
Preconditions: User has address with known city name (e.g. 'New York'); address popup open
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] cart loaded
[ACT] open address selection popup
[WAIT] address list renders
[ACT] type city name in UPPERCASE (e.g. 'NEW YORK') in search input
[ACT] submit search
[WAIT] results update
[ACT] clear search; type city name in lowercase (e.g. 'new york')
[ACT] submit search
[WAIT] results update
Assertions:
[DOM] UPPERCASE search returns same matching address row as lowercase search
[DOM] result count identical for upper and lowercase variants
[STATE] search is case-insensitive as documented in AC-2
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] both keyword variants return same totalCount in currentCustomerAddresses; errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no JS errors
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: uppercase returns 0 results while lowercase returns matches; case-sensitive search filtering applied; inconsistent results

Title: Address Popup — Duplicate Address Guard Shows Warning (AC-13)
Section: Checkout > Shipping > Address Popup > Duplicate Guard
Preconditions: SEED: existing address known; user on Add New address form inside popup; OQ-8 resolved to confirm duplicate-check UI is in scope
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] cart loaded
[ACT] open address selection popup
[WAIT] address list renders
[ACT] click 'Add New' button inside popup
[WAIT] add address form opens
[ACT] fill in the same address details as an existing saved address
[ACT] click save or submit the new address
Assertions:
[DOM] a duplicate warning message or confirmation dialog appears before saving
[DOM] warning message references the duplicate address (not a generic error)
[STATE] duplicate address is NOT saved silently — user is informed
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] checkDuplicateAddress query called before save; isDuplicated = true; errors[] is empty
[CONSOLE] no JS errors on duplicate check
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: duplicate address saved without any warning; checkDuplicateAddress not called; generic error shown instead of duplicate-specific message; OQ-8 blocks this case
Cleanup: Close popup or discard duplicate address
